channel.title: mdspec blog
channel.description: Practical articles for developers on managing markdown specs, AI-era documentation, and shipping without friction.

item.title: Why Markdown Files Are Becoming the Most Important Artifact in AI Development
item.description: Markdown files were once just static documentation. In the AI era, they've become the primary interface between developers and intelligent systems — and most teams aren't treating them that way.

item.title: Managing Markdown Bloat in AI-Era Repos
item.description: AI workflows generate markdown fast. ADRs, specs, meeting notes, agent outputs — it piles up. Here's how to sync it out, keep it searchable, and stop the sprawl.

item.title: The Easy Way to Manage Specs in Your Repo
item.description: From Backstage to Confluence to plain wikis — managing engineering specs is a solved problem with many overcomplicated solutions. Here's a comparison, and a frictionless alternative.

item.title: The Documentation Gap No One Talks About in AI-Speed Development
item.description: AI makes developers faster. Much faster. But task documentation hasn't kept up. Here's what's being lost, why it matters, and how to close the gap before it becomes technical debt you can't see.

item.title: Don't Let AI Discussions Disappear — Write Them to Docs
item.description: Your most valuable architectural decisions are happening in AI chat sessions that vanish when the window closes. Here's how to capture them, store them, and make them searchable across your team's tools.

item.title: How to Auto-Update ClickUp Tasks and Docs Straight from Your GitHub Repo
item.description: Manual ClickUp updates are a tax on developer focus. Here's how to wire your GitHub repo to ClickUp so tasks and docs stay in sync automatically — with and without mdspec.

item.title: The Easiest Way to Sync Markdown Files to S3 from GitHub
item.description: S3 is an ideal archive and distribution layer for engineering docs — durable, cheap, and searchable. Here's how to sync from your repo with minimal setup, using mdspec or a native Actions approach.
